title: Chaetogastra roseotincta
intro: Chaetogastra roseotincta, also known as the "Rose-painted Chaetogastra," is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, Chaetogastra roseotincta has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Chaetogastra roseotincta is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and digestive issues.
